question: To whom did the word of the Lord come through Haggai (Hag 1:1)?
Who was the governor of Judah during the prophecy of Haggai (Hag 1:1)?
Who was the high priest during the prophecy of Haggai (Hag 1:1)?
What do the people say (Hag 1:2)?
options: It is time to build the Lord’s house
It is not time to build the Lord’s house
It is time to build the walls of Jerusalem
It is not time to build the walls of Jerusalem

question: How does God describe their homes (Hag 1:4)?
Why did the Lord object to their living in paneled houses (Hag 1:4)?
options: Because the Lord’s house was in ruins
Because the poor had no homes
Because their brothers were still enslaved
Because the walls had not been rebuilt

question: How have they planted and harvested (Hag 1:6)?
options: They have planted little and harvested the whirlwind
They have planted much and harvested little
They have not planted and yet harvested much
They have planted choice vines but harvested sour grapes

question: What happens when they earn wages (Hag 1:6)?
options: They bury it in the ground
The wages are blown away by the wind
They are taken by their neighbors
They put it in a purse with holes in it

question: What had they expected (Hag 1:9)?
options: The Lord’s presence among his people but he did not come
Prosperity and peace instead poverty and war prevailed
Much but it turned out to be little

question: What are they doing while the Lord’s house lies in ruins (Hag 1:9)?
options: Buying and selling and getting gain
Each is busy with his own house
They are rebuilding the city walls

question: What was Haggai called (Hag 1:13)?
options: The anointed one of the Lord

question: What message did Haggai give to the people who had obeyed the Lord (Hag 1:13)?
options: I will bless and keep you
I will be gracious unto you

question: What did the remnant begin to work on (Hag 1:14)?
options: Laying the foundation of the palace

question: How did the new temple look to the Lord compared to the old (Hag 2:3)?
options: Like a shadow of the former temple

question: What did God say remains among them (Hag 2:5)?
What did the Lord say would come to the temple after he shook all nations (Hag 2:7)?
God said that the glory of this house would be greater than _____ (Hag 2:9)?
options: The glory cloud on Mount Sinai
The glory of the former house
The glory of his heavenly throne

question: What did God say he would grant in this temple (Hag 2:9)?
How can bread or oil become defiled according to Haggai 2 (Hag 2:13)?
options: If a person with leprosy touches it
If a person who has been defiled by touching a dead person touches it
If a person who has eaten defiled meat touches it
If a person who is a Nazirite and touched any leaven touches it

question: What will the Lord do from the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month (Hag 2:19)?
What happened on the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month (Hag 2:18)?
options: They offered their first sacrifice to the Lord
They donated money for the building of the temple
They laid the foundation for the temple

question: Who will the Lord make like a signet ring God has chosen (Hag 2:23)?
What was the Lord call Zerubbabel (Hag 2:23)?
Who does the Lord say his has chosen (Hag 2:23)?
